metadata.title: About Claimondo — Digital Car Damage Claims from Cologne
metadata.description: Platform for car damage claim settlement, founded in Cologne in 2025. Founders: Nicolas Kitta (CEO) + Aaron Sprafke (COO). Certified partner network, no cost under §249 BGB (subject to acknowledgement by the opposing liability insurer).

hero.headline: Complete claim settlement —
hero.subline: We are the platform that gets injured parties what they are entitled to — not just what the insurance company is willing to approve.

definition.p1_continued: digital platform founded in Cologne for the complete settlement of motor vehicle liability claims. The registered office is at
definition.p2: Claimondo coordinates the entire claim settlement process: independent expert report by certified appraisers, legal enforcement through a partner law firm specialised in traffic law, and full payment of all entitlements under §249 BGB (German Civil Code on damage compensation) — repair costs, diminished value (Wertminderung), loss of use (Nutzungsausfall), rental car, and pain and suffering. The service is free of charge for injured parties who are not at fault (subject to acknowledgement by the opposing liability insurer).
definition.p3: The partner network consists of certified appraisers from the public DAT directory, with a focus on North Rhine-Westphalia and nationwide reachability. Appointments are typically available within 48 hours. The legal basis for the right to an independent appraiser is §249 BGB as well as established BGH case law (including VI ZR 65/18, VI ZR 174/24, VI ZR 119/04).

manifesto.heading_pre: In Germany, insurer review services typically cut
manifesto.body: Claimondo exists because this is the norm, not the exception. Insurers commission ControlExpert or K-Expert to produce automated review reports — without ever inspecting the vehicle. UPE surcharges, transfer costs, and diminished value get struck off. Anyone who settles without a lawyer accepts the first reduction. The BGH has ruled in favour of the injured party in multiple decisions — we enforce those rulings.
manifesto.quelle: ¹ Source: NDR documentary "Prüfdienstleister" 2022, Verbraucherzentrale analyses, BGH landmark decisions VI ZR 38/22 ff., VI ZR 65/18, VI ZR 174/24.

werte.heading: Four commitments we are held to

items.text: Our expert appraisers do not work for insurers. Their assessments reflect the reality of damage repair — not insurance tables.
Every claim item under §249 BGB is enforced — repair costs, diminished value, loss of use, pain and suffering. Not just the obvious ones.
Response within 15 minutes. Appointment within 48 hours. Expert report within 48 hours. Payout on average within 6–8 weeks.
Live updates via WhatsApp. Digital case file accessible around the clock. No hold music, no call centre — direct line to the team in Köln.

items.bio_kurz: Nicolas leads Claimondo strategically, overseeing the partner network and investor relations.
items.bio_lang: Nicolas co-founded Claimondo from a simple observation: too many accident victims in Germany sign settlement declarations without realising they are signing away a third of their statutory entitlement. At Claimondo he builds the partner network of DAT-Sachverständige and specialist lawyers — ensuring every client receives the same standard that insurers have long established for their own major accounts.
items.quote: "It's not about who I am, but what I do. That's how you're judged."

items.bio_kurz: Aaron builds the Claimondo platform and oversees operations — from photo-based damage capture to final payout.
items.bio_lang: Aaron's background is in sales and account management at nextright and AdvoScale, where he saw first-hand how much money is lost in every claim file between promise and payout. At Claimondo he built the entire technical platform: AI-assisted damage capture, GPS self-dispatch to the nearest available expert, ZB1 OCR with Imagin visualisation, digital Sicherungsabtretung signing — and ensures that dispatch, experts, lawyers, and the client all see the same data in real time.

signatur.abtretungstext:
ASSIGNMENT DECLARATION (Abtretungserklärung)

I, the undersigned, hereby assign in full to Claimondo GmbH all claims for damages to which I am entitled against the at-fault party and their liability insurer arising from the reported claim.

The assignment covers in particular:
• Claims for compensation of material damage
• Costs for expert reports (Sachverständigengutachten)
• All ancillary damage costs and legal enforcement costs

Claimondo GmbH is entitled to assert and collect the assigned claims in its own name. The assignment is made erfüllungshalber (in satisfaction of the obligation). In the event of non-recovery, the claims revert to the assignor.
signatur.vollmachttext:
POWER OF ATTORNEY & LEGAL MANDATE (Vollmacht & Anwaltsmandat)

I, the undersigned, hereby grant Claimondo GmbH and the lawyers instructed by them full power of attorney to represent me in all matters relating to the reported claim.

The power of attorney covers in particular:
• Out-of-court enforcement of all claims against at-fault parties and insurers
• Judicial enforcement in one's own name and on behalf of others
• Conclusion of settlements and receipt of payments
• Engagement of expert appraisers and other specialists
• Initiation and conduct of enforcement proceedings

This power of attorney remains in effect until expressly revoked in writing.

items.frage: What does a vehicle expert cost after a no-fault accident?
items.antwort: In a no-fault accident with damage exceeding 750 €, you pay 0 €. The opposing liability insurer covers all costs under §249 BGB. Fees based on the BVSK-Honorartabelle range from 550 € to 2,600 € depending on the extent of damage.

items.frage: How quickly can a vehicle expert be on-site?
items.antwort: Certified partner appraisers from the Claimondo network inspect your vehicle nationwide in under 48 hours — usually the same or the following business day.

items.frage: What happens if the insurance reduces the expert report?
items.antwort: Insurers such as HUK, LVM, and AXA typically reduce UPE surcharges, transport costs, and Wertminderung (diminished value) via audit service providers (ControlExpert, K-Expert, DEKRA). However, the BGH supports injured parties in its leading decisions VI ZR 65/18, VI ZR 174/24, and VI ZR 38/22 ff. Our partner law firm for traffic law recovers all reductions in full — including through litigation.

items.frage: Does the free service also apply in cases of partial fault?
items.antwort: Yes. With partial fault, the opposing insurance covers the proportional share. In a 50:50 split, your own comprehensive insurance (Kasko) pays the preferential positions (repair, diminished value, expert fees, towing costs) up to 100 % via the Quotenvorrecht.

items.frage: Do I have to report my vehicle damage to the insurance myself?
items.antwort: No. Do not speak directly with the opposing insurance — they will send their own appraiser (ControlExpert, K-Expert), who systematically reduces claims. Insurer review services typically cut 30–40 % of claims (source: NDR documentary "Prüfdienstleister" 2022, Verbraucherzentrale analyses, BGH landmark rulings VI ZR 38/22 ff. / VI ZR 65/18 / VI ZR 174/24). With an independent certified expert, all BGH-compliant positions are properly documented.

items.frage: How much diminished value can I recover after an accident?
items.antwort: The merkantile Wertminderung (diminished market value) calculated using the Sanden/Danner formula ranges between €500 and €2,500. Rule of thumb: year 1: 25 %, year 2: 20 %, year 3: 15 %, year 4: 10 % of repair costs. No fixed age limit according to BGH VI ZR 357/03.

items.frage: What does the 130 % rule mean in a total loss case?
items.antwort: The 130 % rule (BGH VI ZR 67/91) permits repair costs up to 130 % of the replacement value — provided repairs are carried out professionally in accordance with the expert report and the vehicle continues to be used for 6 months.

items.frage: What is the Werkstattrisiko (workshop risk) under the 2024 BGH rulings?
items.antwort: On 16.01.2024, the BGH clarified in five landmark decisions (VI ZR 38/22, 239/22, 253/22, 266/22, 51/23): injured parties are not required to verify repair invoices themselves. Workshop charges too high or work not carried out → the opposing insurance bears the risk.

items.frage: What is the HIS file and why does it matter?
items.antwort: The HIS file (Hinweis- und Informationssystem — central claims register) stores every reported accident centrally, accessible to all insurers. Anyone who settles on a notional basis and later reports new damage to the same area risks full denial of the claim — recommended evidence preservation: two-photo rule with a current dated newspaper.

items.frage: Are Tesla and electric vehicle damages handled the same way?
items.antwort: Not entirely. In Tesla and other electric vehicles, control units are located beneath sill covers — consequential damage often only emerges months later. DAT/Audatex frequently lack correct assembly times for US vehicles: a standard estimate of €22,000 can turn out to be €48,000 using Tesla original data. We connect you with specialist EV appraisers.

fehler.warum: "We'll take care of everything" means: claims steering to a partner workshop, no independent expert, no diminished value — typically 30–40% less in entitlements due to audit-service reductions (NDR investigative report 2022, Verbraucherzentrale, BGH VI ZR 38/22 ff.).

fehler.warum: "Who pays the piper calls the tune." — Insurance appraisers systematically underestimate damage. Examples: €2,000 instead of €9,000 after dismantling.

fehler.warum: Settlement declarations waive all future claims — including late-onset injuries or hidden vehicle defects.
fehler.besser: Never sign without a lawyer. If the accident was not your fault, the opposing party covers 100% of legal fees.

fehler.titel: Trust a police officer's assessment of "Nothing to it"
fehler.warum: Police officers are not automotive technicians. Hidden damage to chassis rails, control units, or the battery (EVs) goes undetected.

fehler.warum: Without an expert report you forfeit diminished value (in year 2, approx. 20% of repair costs) and have no evidence in later disputes.

fehler.titel: File a notional claim without securing evidence

fehler.titel: Fail to secure video evidence immediately

faqs.frage: What happens if the insurance company reduces the expert report?
faqs.antwort: Insurers typically reduce UPE surcharges, transport costs, adjacent panel repainting, and diminished value via assessment service providers (ControlExpert, K-Expert, DEKRA). Under BGH case law, these items are fully reimbursable (VI ZR 65/18, VI ZR 174/24, VI ZR 38/22 et seq.). Our partner law firm for traffic law recovers the full amount of all reductions — including through litigation.

faqs.frage: What does the priority quota right (Quotenvorrecht) give me in cases of shared liability?
faqs.antwort: With 50:50 shared liability, the opposing insurance pays only 50%. Your own comprehensive cover steps in via the Quotenvorrecht and covers up to 100% of the priority items: repair costs, diminished value, expert fees, and towing costs. The upgrade surcharge (Höherstufungsschaden) can be partially reclaimed from the other party.

faqs.frage: What is merkantile Wertminderung (diminished market value)?
faqs.antwort: Even after a perfect repair, the market value of an accident-damaged vehicle falls — this is the merkantile Wertminderung. The opposing insurance pays it based on the Sanden/Danner formula: year 1: 25%, year 2: 20%, year 3: 15%, year 4: 10% of repair costs. BGH VI ZR 357/03 rejects any fixed age limit.

faqs.frage: Are UPE surcharges and transport costs really reimbursable?
faqs.antwort: Yes — including on a notional (fiktiv) basis. BGH VI ZR 65/18 confirmed UPE surcharges; BGH VI ZR 174/24 confirmed adjacent panel repainting. Insurers still reduce these via assessment reports without inspecting the vehicle. We recover every item through legal counsel.

faqs.frage: Why should I not speak directly with the opposing insurance?
faqs.antwort: Because they deploy their own appraiser (ControlExpert, K-Expert) and systematically reduce claims — assessment services typically cut 30–40% of entitlements (NDR investigative report 2022, Verbraucherzentrale, BGH VI ZR 38/22 et seq.). Simply say: "Thank you, I will exercise my right to engage an independent expert and specialist lawyer of my choice." — and report the claim to us.

fragen.frage: What does Claimondo actually cost?
fragen.antwort: For you as the party not at fault: €0. Expert fees, legal costs, towing costs — all are borne by the opposing liability insurer. This is enshrined in law (§249 BGB). Claimondo is funded exclusively from these line items.

fragen.frage: What do I lose if I simply "take the money" without an expert report?
fragen.antwort: Typically 30–40% of your claim — that is the documented reduction range applied by insurer review services, according to the NDR report "Prüfdienstleister" (2022), Verbraucherzentrale analyses, and BGH landmark rulings (VI ZR 38/22 ff., VI ZR 65/18, VI ZR 174/24). A real-world example: expert-assessed value €11,900 → after insurer reductions approximately €8,000 remains. Lost: VAT (§249 Abs. 2 BGB), UPE surcharges, transport costs, blending costs — and frequently the entire diminished value. Add to that the HIS risk: anyone who accepts payment without a repair invoice and later has another claim risks full rejection by the insurer.

fragen.frage: Do I have to pay upfront?
fragen.antwort: No. Neither for the expert report nor for the lawyer. Both are billed directly to the opposing party. You pay nothing upfront.

fragen.frage: What if the insurance refuses to cover the costs?
fragen.antwort: Our partner law firm responds — with the relevant BGH rulings. If the insurer still reduces the payout, the lawyer takes legal action if necessary. Court costs, too, are borne by the opposing party upon success. If you bear actual contributory fault, we will inform you honestly in advance.

groups.gruppe: Why does the insurance reduce the payout?

fragen.frage: Why does the insurance never pay the full amount of the expert report?
fragen.antwort: Because it can — as long as no one objects. Insurers commission ControlExpert, K-Expert, or DEKRA to produce automated audit reports that cut line items without ever inspecting the vehicle. NDR investigative report: insurance appraiser assessed 2,000 € → independent expert report after dismantling: 9,000 €. BGH rulings stand against this (VI ZR 65/18, VI ZR 174/24) — but only those who object receive what they are owed.

fragen.frage: What do insurers cut most often?
fragen.antwort: 1. Hourly labour rates (citing cheaper workshops), 2. UPE surcharges (recoverable despite BGH VI ZR 65/18), 3. Transport costs (despite BGH entitlement), 4. Blending costs (BGH VI ZR 174/24 from 2025 applies), 5. Expert fees ("excessive"), 6. Diminished value (frequently ignored entirely). Our lawyer knows every one of these line items and pursues them in full.

fragen.frage: HUK, LVM, AXA — does it make a difference?
fragen.antwort: Yes. HUK-COBURG systematically declares expert reports "unusable" and refuses to cover appraiser costs. LVM challenges AI-assisted expert reports. AXA uses supra-regional residual-value exchanges to inflate the residual value (counter-argument: BGH VI ZR 119/04 — the regional market applies). Our lawyer knows each insurer's specific tactics and responds accordingly.

fragen.frage: Why do I need an independent expert?
fragen.antwort: An insurance appraiser works for the insurer. One example: Opel Karl — only scratches visible from the outside; after dismantling: longitudinal frame member displaced, total loss. 7,000 € would simply have been lost without an independent expert report. Only an independent expert calculates diminished value — a workshop does not.

fragen.frage: I have a Tesla or an electric vehicle. Are there any special considerations?
fragen.antwort: Yes — and they are significant. Standard systems (DAT/Audatex) often contain no correct assembly times for US-market vehicles. Real-world example: standard German expert report 22,000 € → with Tesla original data: 48,000 €. Control units beneath sill panels frequently only become a problem months later. For Tesla and electric vehicles: always use a specialist appraiser.

fragen.frage: What is the 130% rule?
fragen.antwort: If repair costs exceed the replacement value by no more than 30%, you may still have your vehicle repaired. Example: replacement value 10,000 € → repair up to 13,000 € possible. Conditions: repair carried out professionally in accordance with the expert report, vehicle used for at least 6 further months. BGH VI ZR 67/91.

fragen.frage: What is the workshop-risk principle and what does it mean for me?
fragen.antwort: BGH rulings of 16.01.2024 (VI ZR 38/22 et al.): if your workshop charges more than estimated or performs work improperly, the opposing insurer bears the risk — not you. You are not required to verify repair costs yourself. A further reason why an independent expert report and a lawyer protect you.

fragen.frage: What is diminished value and will I always receive it?
fragen.antwort: Even after a perfect repair, the market value of an accident-damaged vehicle falls — and the opposing party must compensate that difference. Rule of thumb: year 1 = 25% of repair costs, year 2 = 20%, year 3 = 15%, year 4 = 10%. There is no rigid age limit (BGH VI ZR 357/03) — the OLG Oldenburg recognised diminished value even at 200,000 km. Only an expert calculates it; a workshop does not.

fragen.frage: Rental car or loss of use — which is better for me?
fragen.antwort: It depends on your situation. Commuters who rely on their car daily: rental car. If you have a second vehicle or liability is unclear: loss of use (Sanden/Danner table, group A to L, approx. 23 € to 175 € per day). For lengthy repairs, loss of use is often the more favourable option. We will advise you specifically on this.

fragen.frage: What are the most common mistakes after an accident?
fragen.antwort: 1. Engaging with the opposing insurer's "claims management" service. 2. Accepting their appraiser. 3. Signing settlement declarations prematurely — this extinguishes ALL future claims (including late-onset damage). 4. Relying on "there's nothing to it" (police officers are not technicians). 5. Having repairs done without an expert report — diminished value is forfeited. 6. Failing to preserve video evidence — the overwrite period is often 3–4 weeks.

fragen.frage: The insurer tells me: "We will take care of everything." What does that mean?
fragen.antwort: Claims steering. The insurer sends you their own appraiser, refers you to a partner workshop, and saves considerably in the process. Your answer: "Thank you, but I am exercising my right to an independent expert and a specialist lawyer of my own choosing." That is exactly what Claimondo is here for.

fragen.frage: I have personal injury claims. What should I be aware of?
fragen.antwort: See a doctor immediately — even if you "feel fine." Whiplash injuries often manifest with a delay. Have your MdE (Minderung der Erwerbsfähigkeit — reduced earning capacity) documented. Do not sign any settlement declaration — doing so extinguishes all claims for future complications. Compensation for pain and suffering: whiplash injuries €200–€70,000, arm fractures €1,600–€65,000.

fragen.frage: What about hit-and-run accidents?
fragen.antwort: The Verkehrsopferhilfe e.V. (VOH) steps in — but vehicle property damage is unfortunately NOT covered; for that you need fully comprehensive insurance. Compensation for pain and suffering is only available for "particularly severe injuries." Statute of limitations: 3 years. Important: immediately secure video footage from petrol stations, car parks, etc., and preserve any dashcam recordings.

fragen.frage: How quickly can I get an appointment with the appraiser?
fragen.antwort: At Claimondo, typically within 48 hours — same-day appointments are possible for urgent damage. The expert comes to you — workshop, home, or workplace, whichever suits you. You do not need to bring the vehicle anywhere yourself. For major damage (total loss, vehicle transport required), the appointment may take longer, but we coordinate that too.

fragen.frage: How long does the appraisal itself take?
fragen.antwort: The on-site inspection takes 30–90 minutes depending on the extent of the damage. The written expert report is available within 48 hours for standard damage, or 3–5 business days for Tesla/electric vehicles or total losses requiring dismantling. Without the report, the lawyer cannot settle the claim — speed is critical here, which is exactly why we work with certified appraisers.

fragen.frage: When do I receive my money?
fragen.antwort: Repair costs are typically paid by the insurance within 4–8 weeks of receiving the expert report. Diminished value, rental car, and loss of use usually follow within the same period. If there are delays or reductions, our lawyer writes — if the insurance still reduces the payout, we litigate. Nationwide average: 6–8 weeks from accident to full payment.

fragen.frage: Do I need to wash or repair my car before the inspection?
fragen.antwort: No — quite the opposite. Leave the vehicle exactly as it was after the accident. Dirt, glass splinters, and hanging parts are all evidence of the damage. Do not attempt any dismantling yourself either — some damage only becomes visible when the appraiser opens up the vehicle (e.g. longitudinal frame members, control units).

fragen.frage: What is DAT-Expert and why does it matter?
fragen.antwort: DAT (Deutsche Automobil Treuhand) is Germany's leading vehicle valuation database. Our appraisers use the DAT calculation system — insurance companies accept these reports without disputing the underlying data. Other systems (e.g. Audatex) are also recognised, but DAT is the market leader and our default choice.

fragen.frage: What sets a Claimondo appraiser apart from DEKRA?
fragen.antwort: DEKRA and TÜV are traditional testing organisations — they primarily carry out vehicle inspections (HU/AU). They also produce accident reports, but DEKRA is frequently commissioned directly by insurers and is therefore not independent. Our partners are freelance, certified appraisers who work exclusively for the injured party — no parallel insurance mandates.

fragen.frage: Can you help with Tesla, Polestar, or other electric vehicles?
fragen.antwort: Yes, but Tesla and electric vehicle appraisals require specialist expertise. We route such cases specifically to appraisers with Tesla/Polestar diagnostic access. Important: do not accept the standard DAT calculation schema for Tesla — labour times and original parts prices must be calculated via Tesla Service Plus; otherwise you may lose €20,000 or more.

fragen.frage: My car is over 10 years old — can I still get an appraisal?
fragen.antwort: Yes. Older vehicles still have residual value, often more than a workshop inspection might suggest. For classic cars (over 30 years old), collector's value also comes into play — standard appraisers frequently miscalculate this. In such cases we refer you to specialist appraisers with classic car experience. BGH position: no fixed age limit for diminished value claims (VI ZR 357/03).

fragen.frage: Do I really need a lawyer? Can't I deal directly with the insurance?
fragen.antwort: You can deal directly with the insurance — but you will typically lose 30–40% of your claim through reductions made by their assessment services (NDR report 2022, Verbraucherzentrale, BGH VI ZR 38/22 et seq.). Insurers routinely reduce diminished value, UPE surcharges, transport costs, and more. A lawyer knows these tactics. Since the opposing party bears all legal costs in cases of third-party fault, the calculation is straightforward: €0 risk (pursuant to §249 BGB, subject to acknowledgement of liability), BGH-compliant maximum claims. A lawyer is almost always worthwhile.

fragen.frage: What will the lawyer cost me?
fragen.antwort: In cases of third-party fault: €0. Legal fees form part of the damages under §249 BGB and are covered in full by the opposing party's liability insurer — RVG fees at their full statutory rate. Even in litigation, the opposing party bears court and legal costs upon success. Where contributory fault applies, costs are apportioned accordingly — we will inform you honestly in advance.

fragen.frage: What is a Sicherungsabtretung — and is it safe?
fragen.antwort: In a Sicherungsabtretung (§398 BGB), you assign your claim against the insurer — up to the amount of the appraisal fee — to the appraiser. You sign once; the appraiser then settles directly with the insurer. You bear no insolvency risk and no advance-payment risk. Standard practice across the entire industry, confirmed by the BGH.

fragen.frage: How long do I have to assert my claims?
fragen.antwort: The statute of limitations for damages claims arising from road accidents is 3 years (§195 BGB), running from the date of knowledge of the damage. Compensation for pain and suffering arising from delayed consequences can be claimed at a later stage. Important: do not sign anything described as a "settlement" or "general release" — doing so extinguishes ALL claims, including unknown future complications. If in doubt: come to us first, then sign.

fragen.frage: What happens to my data?
fragen.antwort: All data is processed in Germany in compliance with the GDPR (servers in Frankfurt, Supabase EU). Data is shared only with: the assigned expert (for the assessment), the partner law firm (for the settlement), and the opposing insurance (for payment). No advertising data sharing, no sales to third parties. Full access and deletion at any time by email to datenschutz@claimondo.de.

fragen.frage: Are photos and documents uploaded securely?
fragen.antwort: Yes — encrypted HTTPS connection, storage in Supabase Storage with row-level security. Only the assigned expert, lawyer, and you yourself can view the documents. Police reports, copies of ID documents, and vehicle registration certificates remain within the protected case — nothing is made public or shared with unauthorised third parties.

fragen.frage: Will I be stored in the HIS database (Hinweis- und Informationssystem der Versicherer)?
fragen.antwort: In the case of a no-fault accident: no — only injured parties who themselves file an insurance claim are stored there. You are asserting a damages claim against the at-fault party, which is not HIS-relevant. It is different if you settle fictitiously without repair and later have another claim — HIS can then block your payout. So it is advisable to actually carry out the repair.

fragen.frage: My vehicle is a total loss — what now?
fragen.antwort: In the event of a total loss, you receive the replacement value minus the residual value. The residual value is determined on the regional market (BGH VI ZR 119/04 — NOT a residual-value exchange 200 km away). The 130% rule: if repair costs up to 30% above the replacement value, you may still have the vehicle repaired — provided the repair is carried out professionally and the vehicle is used for at least 6 months afterwards. Practical example: replacement value €8,000 → repair up to €10,400 is permissible.

fragen.frage: What about a rental car during repairs?
fragen.antwort: You are entitled to an equivalent rental car for the entire duration of repairs (BGH line: one class lower is acceptable with partial reimbursement). Alternatively: loss-of-use flat rate per the Sanden/Danner table (Group A to L, approx. €23 to €175/day). For commuters, a rental car is almost always the better option. We advise on your specific situation — insurers frequently cut this to a "comparable class," which is not always justified.

fragen.frage: I have a claim involving a company vehicle. Does this work too?
fragen.antwort: Yes. The claim settlement process runs analogously — the registered keeper is the company, and a power of attorney from management is required. Important: companies entitled to input tax deduction settle on a NET basis (no VAT entitlement under §249 Abs. 2 BGB). Private individuals settle on a gross basis. Loss of use is also available for company vehicles — the Sanden/Danner rates apply equally.

fragen.frage: What if fault is unclear?
fragen.antwort: Where fault is unclear, we assess the evidence — police report, witnesses, dashcam footage, traffic diagram. In a 50/50 split, each side bears 50% of their own damage. Common scenario: a rear-end collision is typically 100:0 (the driver who rear-ends is fully liable), a lane change with oncoming traffic is often 70:30. We give an honest assessment — no promises we cannot keep.

fragen.frage: What is the quota priority (Quotenvorrecht) and how does it help me?
fragen.antwort: In cases of shared fault (e.g. 50:50), the opposing insurance only covers the proportional share — in a 50:50 split, that is just half. However, through the Quotenvorrecht, your own comprehensive insurance steps in and covers up to 100% of the priority positions: repair costs, diminished value, expert fees, and towing costs. Loss of use and the flat-rate incidental expenses are not priority positions — these run through the quota only.

fragen.frage: Example calculation: 50:50 shared fault with €10,000 repair — what do I receive?
fragen.antwort: Repair €10,000 + diminished value €1,000 + expert report €800 + towing €500 = €12,300 in priority positions. The opposing third-party liability insurance pays 50% = €6,150. Your comprehensive insurance covers the remaining €6,150 (Quotenvorrecht) — in practice you bear virtually no out-of-pocket cost. The upgrade damage (increase in comprehensive insurance premiums) can be reclaimed proportionally from the opposing party.

fragen.frage: Do I lose my no-claims discount through the Quotenvorrecht?
fragen.antwort: Not automatically. The premium increase resulting from use of your comprehensive cover (upgrade damage) is claimable as consequential loss against the opposing party — pro rata. In a 50:50 split, the opposing party bears 50% of the lifetime additional premiums. At 70:30, correspondingly more. Our partner law firm for traffic law calculates this precisely and recovers it.

fragen.frage: When is the insurance allowed to refer me to a cheaper workshop?
fragen.antwort: For vehicles under 3 years old: never — authorised dealer labour rates always apply (BGH VI ZR 53/09). For vehicles over 3 years old: only if the service history is not maintained without gaps. A complete service history — including from previous owners — continues to entitle you to authorised dealer rates. Without a complete service history, the insurer may refer you to a comparable independent workshop.

fragen.frage: What if the insurer's "partner workshop" is 50 km away?
fragen.antwort: The insurer may not refer you to a workshop that is unreasonable in terms of distance or quality. If the distance exceeds 25–30 km, or the workshop lacks comparable facilities (e.g. Tesla-certified, body alignment rig), the referral is invalid. You retain the right to authorised dealer rates at a workshop of your choice.

fragen.frage: The insurer wants to impose a residual-value offer from the internet on me — do I have to accept it?
fragen.antwort: No. Residual value is determined by the regional market — cross-regional internet salvage exchanges are irrelevant (BGH VI ZR 119/04). A buyer from 300 km away may offer more, but as a private individual you cannot practically accept that offer. Your appraiser obtains 3 concrete regional residual-value offers — that is the reliable basis.

fragen.frage: What if the insurer still insists on the cross-regional residual value?
fragen.antwort: In that case, the partner law firm for traffic law responds with BGH VI ZR 119/04 and the 3 regional offers from the expert report. As a rule, the insurance then accepts the regional values. In the event of a dispute, we take the matter to the competent Landgericht — case law is unambiguously on your side.

faq.eyebrow: Frequently asked questions about the process
faq.heading: Answers in under 60 seconds

faqs.frage: How quickly is the first callback?
faqs.antwort: Within 15 minutes during business hours — 08:00 to 20:00. Outside those hours, the next working day morning. You can write via WhatsApp at any time.

faqs.frage: Do I have to speak to the opposing insurance?
faqs.antwort: No — and you shouldn't. As soon as you report your claim with us, our partner law firm for traffic law takes over all communication. If the opposing insurance contacts you directly: simply forward it to us.

faqs.frage: What happens if the insurance rejects my expert report?
faqs.antwort: That happens frequently. ControlExpert / K-Expert produce audit reports without inspecting the vehicle and systematically reduce UPE, transport costs, and Wertminderung (diminished value). Our partner law firm for traffic law responds with BGH references (VI ZR 65/18, VI ZR 174/24, VI ZR 38/22 et seq.) and recovers the deductions in full. If necessary through litigation — the opposing party also pays the legal and court costs.

faqs.frage: Can I track progress myself?
faqs.antwort: Yes, live in the Claimondo portal: the expert's location, status of the repair estimate, incoming insurance responses, projected payment date. Also available as a mobile app with push notifications on every status change.

faqs.frage: Do I need to pay anything upfront?
faqs.antwort: No. Sicherungsabtretung under §398 BGB transfers the claim for the expert's fee directly to the appraiser — who settles with the insurance. Repair via the workshop works the same way. In a no-fault accident you pay €0 out of pocket (under §249 BGB, subject to acknowledgement by the opposing liability insurer).

faqs.frage: What if I don't live in NRW?
faqs.antwort: No problem. Our main focus is NRW, but the partner network covers 72 major German cities — Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, Frankfurt, Stuttgart, Hannover, Leipzig, Dresden, Bremen, Kiel and more. City pages with local Landgericht / bar association / BVSK-Honorartabelle range at /kfz-gutachter/<stadt>.

faqs.frage: How high is the platform commission?

faqs.frage: Am I tied to Claimondo, or can I continue taking my own assignments?
faqs.antwort: You remain self-employed. Your own direct assignments, BVSK membership, insurer listings — all remain unchanged. Claimondo is an additional channel, not an exclusive commitment.

faqs.frage: What requirements do I need to meet?
faqs.antwort: At least one recognised qualification: DAT-Expert, BVSK membership, IHK certificate, or public appointment (öbuv). Additionally: valid professional liability insurance, GoBD-compliant invoicing, and an active location in Germany.

faqs.frage: How long does onboarding take?
faqs.antwort: After your region is activated: 7 to 14 working days. Steps include qualification verification, contract signing, portal setup, and a 30-minute live onboarding call. After that, the first assignments go live.

faqs.frage: What software is used?
faqs.antwort: Orders, appointments, evidence photos, and expert report dispatch are managed via the Claimondo SV Portal (web + native app). DAT-SilverDAT integration is ready. Your own expert software (Audatex, Combiplus) can continue to run in parallel — we import the finished PDF.

faqs.frage: How are invoicing and payment handled?
faqs.antwort: Claimondo handles invoicing directly with the opposing liability insurer under §249 BGB. You receive your fee via SEPA on standard payment terms — 14 days after expert report receipt, regardless of any insurer delay.

faqs.frage: Can I adjust my region later?
faqs.antwort: Yes. You can update your radius and postcode list in the portal at any time. For expansions, we check whether the neighbouring region is available — reductions take effect immediately.

faqs.frage: What happens to customer data? GDPR-compliant?
faqs.antwort: All customer data is stored GDPR-compliant on German servers (Supabase Frankfurt). You receive case data exclusively for the duration of processing; deletion is automated after case closure plus the statutory retention period.

gegnerische_vs.hero_badge: § 249 BGB · § 286 BGB default · BGH VI ZR 280/22
gegnerische_vs.hero_h1: The opposing insurance won't pay — what you can do now
gegnerische_vs.hero_intro: Delays, reductions, or silence are not inevitable. Once a reasonable review period has passed, the opposing liability insurer is in default (Verzug) — and owes the full claim amount plus interest. If the accident was not your fault, we enforce this on your behalf — 0 € out of pocket.
gegnerische_vs.hero_cta_primary: Get your case reviewed for free
gegnerische_vs.antwort_h2: In short: you hold the stronger hand
gegnerische_vs.antwort_p: In a no-fault accident, the opposing liability insurer owes full compensation under §249 BGB. If they fail to pay — or pay only in part — after a reasonable review period (typically 4–6 weeks from receipt of complete documentation), they enter default (§286 BGB), triggering interest at 5 percentage points above the base rate (§288 Abs. 1 BGB). Engaging a lawyer to enforce your claim is itself a recoverable cost under the principle of full restitution.
antwort_bullets: Full compensation under §249 BGB — not just a fraction
Default interest from the moment of default (§288 BGB)
Legal costs are borne by the opposing party — not you
You don't negotiate yourself — the partner law firm handles it
gegnerische_vs.taktiken_h2: The typical letters — and what they really mean
gegnerische_vs.taktiken_p: Insurer review services rely on recurring boilerplate. Recognise your letter? Each line leads to a legal classification with the appropriate counter-argument.

taktiken.bedeutung: Delay without a deadline. Default still occurs once the reasonable review period has elapsed.
A settlement below your actual loss — one you are not obliged to accept.
A blanket reduction to a "standard rate" — often without any sound legal basis.

taktiken.brief: "You are not entitled to loss of use."
taktiken.bedeutung: If you forgo a rental car, the claim for Nutzungsausfall (loss of use) generally still stands.

taktiken.brief: "We will not pay for diminished value."
taktiken.bedeutung: Merkantile Wertminderung (diminished market value) is a standalone, recoverable head of loss.
Pushes toward a total-loss settlement instead of repair — the 130% rule under BGH case law frequently applies.

The appraiser provided or paid by the opposing insurer is not neutral — they serve the interest of keeping the settlement low. Commission your own expert report before repairs begin (evidence preservation).

unverschuldeter_unfall.hero_badge: § 249 BGB · Naturalrestitution · Free choice of repair shop & expert
unverschuldeter_unfall.hero_h1: Accident without fault — what you are entitled to now
unverschuldeter_unfall.hero_intro: Anyone not at fault for an accident has the right to full restoration (§ 249 BGB). From the expert report to pain and suffering compensation, the opposing liability insurer covers everything — 0 € out of pocket for you.
unverschuldeter_unfall.antwort_h2: The principle: you should be in the same position as if the accident never happened
unverschuldeter_unfall.antwort_p: § 249 BGB requires Naturalrestitution — the full restoration of your pre-accident situation. This gives rise to your individual claims, as well as three important freedoms: free choice of expert, free choice of repair shop (BGH VI ZR 53/09), and free choice of lawyer. No one may refer you to the insurer's appraiser or their "partner network."

unfall_was_tun.hero_h1: Accident — What to Do as an Injured Party?
unfall_was_tun.hero_intro: The first minutes determine your evidence — the next weeks determine your money. This guide walks you through immediate measures, liability by accident type, and all applicable claims. When the accident is not your fault, the opposing party covers the expert report and legal fees — €0 for you.

unfall_was_tun.antwort_p: First secure people and evidence, make no admission of fault, and commission your own expert before any repairs begin. When the accident is not your fault, you are entitled to full restitution (§ 249 BGB), enforceable directly against the opposing party's liability insurer (§ 115 VVG). Legal and expert fees are borne by the opposing party — you bear no financial risk.

unfall_was_tun.sofort_h2: Immediate steps at the accident scene
unfall_was_tun.sofort_p: Follow these steps in order — from protecting yourself to securing evidence:

sofort.name: Secure the accident scene
sofort.text: Switch on hazard lights, put on your high-visibility vest, place the warning triangle at a sufficient distance (built-up areas ~50 m, rural roads ~100 m, motorways ~150–200 m).

sofort.name: Attend to the injured, call emergency services 112
sofort.text: If anyone is injured, provide first aid and call emergency services (112) first. Failure to render assistance is a criminal offence — observe your own safety.

sofort.name: Call the police (110)
sofort.text: In cases of personal injury, significant property damage, disputed circumstances, hit-and-run, suspected alcohol/drug use, or involvement of rental vehicles/foreign parties. When in doubt, call.

sofort.name: Secure evidence
sofort.text: Take photos from multiple angles (overview, final positions, damage, licence plates, skid marks) and prepare an accident sketch.

sofort.name: Exchange details
sofort.text: Record the name, address, licence plate, liability insurance and policy number of the opposing party — but do not make any admission of fault.

sofort.name: Identify witnesses
sofort.text: Record the names and phone numbers of uninvolved witnesses — they are often decisive in disputed liability cases.

sofort.name: Engage your own expert appraiser & lawyer
sofort.text: Before any repairs begin, commission your own independent expert appraiser (evidence preservation) and have your claims enforced through a road traffic law firm.

unfall_was_tun.unfallskizze_link: Create an accident sketch — free printable template
unfall_was_tun.typen_h2: Who is liable? The 6 most common accident types
unfall_was_tun.typen_p: Liability depends on the course of the accident. Tap for the legal assessment with BGH reference:

typen.haftung: The driver who rear-ends is generally fully liable — the prima facie evidence rule applies (insufficient following distance or inattention).

typen.titel: Failure to yield right of way
typen.haftung: Anyone who disregards "right before left" or a yield sign bears overwhelming liability.
The left-turning driver typically bears primary fault — they must give way to oncoming vehicles (§ 9 StVO).
The driver who changes lanes is regularly liable in a collision — the duty of care falls on the one who changes.
Shared liability is common: car parks require walking pace and mutual consideration.
Running a red light means full liability — proof is often established through witnesses or the signal phase.

unfall_was_tun.rechte_h2: Your claims after a no-fault accident
unfall_was_tun.rechte_p: You are entitled to be placed in the position you would have been in without the accident (Naturalrestitution, §249 BGB). This gives rise to a whole bundle of claims — from repair and diminished value to loss of use and compensation for pain and suffering:

unfall_was_tun.versicherung_h2: What the opposing insurance is not telling you
unfall_was_tun.versicherung_p: The opposing insurer represents its own interests, not yours. You are not obliged to accept their appraiser or their first offer — and reductions are often unjustified:

faqs.frage: What do I need to do immediately after an accident?
faqs.antwort: Secure the scene (hazard lights, high-visibility vest, warning triangle), attend to anyone injured and call 112 if necessary, call the police (110) if needed, gather evidence (photos and accident sketch), record the other party's details — without admitting fault — and instruct your own expert before any repairs.

faqs.frage: When must the police attend an accident?
faqs.antwort: At the latest in the event of personal injury, significant property damage, a dispute about the course of events, hit-and-run, suspected alcohol or drug use, or the involvement of a foreign or rental vehicle. When in doubt, always call — the police report secures evidence.

faqs.frage: May I admit fault at the accident scene?
faqs.antwort: No. Do not admit fault and do not sign any pre-drafted statements. Record only what you observed — liability is determined later on the basis of the evidence.

faqs.frage: Do I need a lawyer — and what will it cost me?
faqs.antwort: In the case of a no-fault accident, the costs of legal representation are a necessary part of restoring you to your prior position and are borne by the opposing liability insurer (BGH VI ZR 235/13). You incur no out-of-pocket costs — those who negotiate without a lawyer, however, frequently accept offers that are too low.

faqs.frage: What does the opposing insurance pay?
faqs.antwort: In the case of a no-fault accident, the full loss pursuant to §249 BGB: repair or replacement, diminished value, loss of use or rental car, expert and legal costs, and — in the event of personal injury — compensation for pain and suffering. Enforced via the direct claim (§115 VVG).

faqs.frage: How much time do I have to enforce my claims?
faqs.antwort: The standard limitation period is 3 years (§195 BGB) from the end of the year in which you became aware; for personal injury claims up to 30 years (§199 para. 2 BGB). Evidence should, however, be secured immediately.
